methodology.rationale: Non-parametric, robust to outliers and non-normality, appropriate for small N. Does not require distributional assumptions.
methodology.insufficient_data_threshold: N < 10 flagged as insufficient for evaluation

classification_criteria.moderate_signal: 0.3 <= abs(rho) <= 0.5 for at least one multiple
classification_criteria.insufficient_data: N < 10 or fewer than 3 paired observations

sample_notes.consistent_sub_sample: N=9 firms with all 3 multiples. All correlations on this sample have N<10, so all are flagged as insufficient_data per methodology requirements. Raw rho values are provided for directional reference.
sample_notes.full_sample: Per-multiple samples ranging from N=10 (P/DE) to N=19 (P/FRE). Provides more statistical power but sacrifices cross-multiple comparability since different firm sets are used for each multiple.
sample_notes.recommendation: Use full-sample results for driver classification given the small consistent sub-sample. Flag stable_value_driver classifications as provisional where any constituent multiple has N<15.

bam_bn_treatment.issue: BAM (Brookfield Asset Management) and BN (Brookfield Corporation) share identical AUM figures (FEAUM=$603B, Total AUM=$1,100B) because BAM is the asset-light manager spun out of BN. Including both would violate the independence assumption.
bam_bn_treatment.resolution: BN excluded from all correlation samples. BN has only P/DE (17.5x) and no P/FRE or EV/FEAUM, so it would not have appeared in the consistent sub-sample regardless. BAM is retained as the pure-play AM entity.
bam_bn_treatment.impact: Minimal. BN was only present in 1-of-3 multiples and would not have entered the consistent sub-sample.
